$description: Design tokens extracted from the live codebase (app/globals.css @theme block + values observed directly in components). Format follows the W3C Design Tokens Community Group draft ($value/$type/$description). Where the codebase has a real, named token (a CSS variable in @theme), it's extracted as-is. Where a value is only ever used ad hoc (e.g. a one-off text-[36px]), it's still listed here as an observed value so the scale is visible, but it is NOT yet a formal reusable token in the code — treat those as a starting point to rationalize, not gospel.

accent.$description: Primary brand blue. Source: --color-accent in app/globals.css. Used on the 'Start Your Project' CTA, active blog filter pills, primary buttons generally.

highlight.$description: A second, distinct blue used only on /explorations (active Grid/Zoom toggle button, small square tag markers). Not defined in app/globals.css — it's a literal hex used directly in app/explorations and app/blog components. FLAG: this is close to but different from color.brand.accent (#004aeb vs #0658FC) — confirm with design whether these should be unified into one brand blue or are intentionally distinct.

muted-65.$description: Secondary text on dark backgrounds (e.g. hero subheadings). Source: --color-muted-65.

label-grey.$description: Small uppercase labels (e.g. 'Filters:', 'Display:', 'Trusted by top YC companies'). Source: --color-label-grey.

button-dark.$description: Translucent fill for secondary/dark buttons (e.g. default 'Chat on Telegram' button). Source: --color-button-dark.

hairline.$description: 0.5px divider lines used throughout case study, blog, and explorations pages. Source: --color-hairline.

surface-navy.$description: Observed literal #04112A used once, as the fill for the blog post author-info box (components/blog/BlogPostPage.tsx). Not in @theme. REPLACE ME: confirm whether this is a real reusable 'card surface' color or a one-off — if reusable, promote it into app/globals.css's @theme block.

display.$description: Primary body/display typeface — Inter Display, self-hosted via next/font/local. Applied globally on <body>. Source: --font-display.

button.$description: Button label typeface only (Instrument Sans via next/font/google). Source: --font-button.

display-tight.$description: Tight tracking for display headings. Source: .tracking-display utility in app/globals.css — expressed in em (-1.1px at the 30px desktop H1 size) so it scales proportionally at smaller sizes.

size.$description: Ad hoc pixel sizes observed via text-[Npx] across components — NOT a formalized/rationalized type scale in the code today. 17 distinct sizes exist for headings and body text combined. REPLACE ME: this list is a starting point for defining a real scale (e.g. xs/sm/base/lg/xl/2xl...); it is not yet load-bearing anywhere as shared tokens.

12.$description: Small uppercase labels, FAQ question text base size context, pill labels.

14.$description: Case sidebar body copy, secondary meta text.

16.$description: Hero subheading, FAQ answer/question body text, section paragraphs.

24.$description: Mobile-size H2/section heading (MoreProjects, case study Q&A headings, FAQ heading base size).

30.$description: LeftNav hero H1, desktop default size.

36.$description: Blog page H1 and post title, mobile size.

64.$description: Blog listing page H1 ('Articles'), desktop; case study hero-scale headings.

spacing.$description: No custom spacing scale is defined in @theme — the project relies entirely on Tailwind's default 4px-based scale (space-N = N * 0.25rem). base below is that unit; there is nothing else to extract here as a project-specific token.

base.$description: Tailwind's default spacing unit. Common gaps observed in the codebase: 4px (gap-1), 8px (gap-2), 12px (gap-3), 16px (gap-4), 20px (gap-5), 24px (gap-6/p-6), 32px (gap-8/py-8).

sm.$description: Default rectangular buttons (CtaButtons, MobileMenu panel links).

full.$description: Fully circular elements (footer social-list separator dot, explorations lightbox nav arrow buttons).

shadow.$description: No box-shadow usage found anywhere in the codebase — the visual language is flat (color + hairline borders only, no elevation/shadow system). REPLACE ME if a shadow scale is needed for a future component (e.g. modals, dropdowns) — none exists to extract today.

desk.$description: 1200px. The site's only custom breakpoint (Tailwind's default sm/md/lg/xl/2xl are unused in favor of this single 'desk' cutoff). Two-column layouts (sidebar + content) switch here across the whole site. Source: --breakpoint-desk in app/globals.css.
